title: OCPBUGS-81187: proxy: Verify *either* toplevel or target
body:
Carrying #2400 to `release-1.22` for OpenShift 4.22.  We'll want a dev-branch fix too, likely in the neighborhood of the code containers/container-libs#758 was touching (but with #2400's either-or behavior, to avoid breaking folks who only sign the manifest-list).  But for now, focusing on healing the breakage in OpenShift, and we'll come back for the development branch(es?) later.

The rebase was straightforward, just adjusting `github.com/containers/image/v5/signature` to `go.podman.io/image/v5/signature` to catch up with #2688, and `"skopeo"` to `skopeoBinary` to catch up with #2703.

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
rerolled with 6d2b8e348 -> 86d61be36 to catch up with #2554's `runTestGetManifestAndConfig` -> `runTestMetadataAPIs`.
Er but that PR never got merged...I think we need to merge into main first, right?

Correct.   We need to merge into main first, and that PR needs rebasing as of this moment.  If we want to get this into RHEL 9.8, we will need a Jira card to make that happen.  Our deadline for RHEL 9.8 is April 15, I think RHEL's is April 22 for the drop dead.  To complicate matters, the RHEL QE team is not currently available to test anything for a RHEL 9.8 submission, so I don't think we will be able to get this into 9.8 even if all the other ducks line up in a row.

> But for now, focusing on healing the breakage in OpenShift, and we'll come back for the development branch(es?) later.

There's some context I'm missing here but...is there a reason whatever this is doing can't sign the manifest list? Is it because the OCP release image peels manifest lists or something? Kind of guessing here

86d61be36 -> b84fd255f rerolls to remove a more-recent `cacheTargetManifest` that was added in #2554.  Now at least [validate is passing][1].

> I think we need to merge into main first, right?

We will need `main`.  I don't think we need it _first_.  From my [pull-opening comment][2]

> We'll want a dev-branch fix too, likely in the neighborhood of the code containers/container-libs#758 was touching (but with https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2400's either-or behavior, to avoid breaking folks who only sign the manifest-list). But for now, focusing on healing the breakage in OpenShift, and we'll come back for the development branch(es?) later.

> We need to merge into main first, and that PR needs rebasing as of this moment.

Is this logic even staying in `skopeo`?  I thought it was moving to `container-libs`, see the code containers/container-libs#758 was touching.

> If we want to get this into RHEL 9.8, we will need a Jira card to make that happen.

I have OCPBUGS-81187.  Feel free to shift it to another project if necessary, or clone, or whatever.

> Our deadline for RHEL 9.8 is April 15, 

This is why I'm skipping main for now.  [OCP 4.22.0-ec.5 is using RHEL CoreOS 9.8.20260314-0 which uses `skopeo-2:1.22.0-2.el9`][3], which is why I assumed the 1.22 branch was the way to get into OCP 4.22.

> To complicate matters, the RHEL QE team is not currently available to test anything for a RHEL 9.8 submission, so I don't think we will be able to get this into 9.8 even if all the other ducks line up in a row.

I hear that folks are trying to find some additional QE that can try to fill these kinds of gaps, but I dunno if OCP QE can drop into RHEL-land cold and be able to turn things around in time.  I'll try to figure out who's available OCP-side, if you can figure out who they'd need to talk to RHEL side.

> ...is there a reason whatever this is doing can't sign the manifest list? 

That's the direction I'd been expection to go, until I found [this `oc-mirror` code][4] that isn't aware that manifest-lists might be signed.  We can sign the manifest list _and_ patch `oc-mirror`.  But what else might we be missing?  Having signers signs both manifest-lists _and_ per-arch shards, and also having consumers find at least per-arch shards sufficient seemed like a less brittle approach for now, and then we can work on manifest-list signatures and their distribution tooling more calmly (e.g. over a few months, instead of the few-week window we have left before 4.22 is near-frozen).

[1]: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2847/checks?check_run_id=70867086950
[2]: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2847#issue-4240403753
[3]: https://amd64.ocp.releases.ci.openshift.org/releasestream/4-dev-preview/release/4.22.0-ec.4
[4]: https://github.com/openshift/oc-mirror/blob/039f0f834173aa89fa9c8c1d4f40b6c4ae4a426a/internal/pkg/signature/cosign_tag_based_signatures.go#L71-L78

[testing-farm:fedora-eln-x86_64:skopeo-eln][1] sounds like a harness failure before we got far enough in to excercise this code?

```
[2026-04-10 23:21:33] [E] [Fedora-eln:x86_64:/plans] guest setup failed: Failure during Ansible playbook execution
```

Similar with [`testing-farm:fedora-eln-aarch64:skopeo-eln`][2].

[1]: https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/ae2b0c0b-2eab-42b6-a588-14f1f014b8e7
[2]: https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/b0a7347d-f56d-41f6-923b-d829ca576e4b
I have triggered a re-run of the "testing-farm" tests (but they are not set up as blocking anyway).
https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/765 is the corresponding mainline update (with a few changes added, to handle the review comments above and linters in container-libs).
At least one test is flaking.  I have restarted.

Poking at the CI failures: * [`cross`](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2847/checks?check_run_id=71120904027) links [View more details on Cirrus CI](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4761411403907072) links [Cirrus CI shuts down on June 1st 2026](https://cirruslabs.org/). And also [`Failed to start: INTERNAL: Compute engine internal error! null`](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5551984557686784)? I'm not entirely sure what I'm looking at here. Sounds like infra, and not my change? Also, any plan to migrate off Cirrus CI by that shutdown date? * [`Skopeo Test`](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6677884464529408?logs=setup#L1) same? Doesn't seem like there's much information in these links, e.g. opening the `Clone` tab is blank:
@wking The Cirrus going-away bit was the other shoe that dropped on the same day we learned about the QE status.  It was a {your favorite explative(s) here} kind of week last week.

FWIW, I checked with our PO, @ddarrah, and he's OK with moving forward with this.

I'm trying to wrap my head around these changes a bit.  It looks like in the main branch, @mtrmac has moved most of the changes to containers-lib with https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/765.  

Is there a reason to do the changes here in Skopeo rather than backporting https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/765 into the container-libs podman-5.8 branch?

> Is there a reason to do the changes here in Skopeo rather than backporting https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/765 into the container-libs podman-5.8 branch?

What's landed there so far is a lot bigger change (though mostly mechanical) and I don't think has seen widespread testing. It seems more likely to involve larger backports.

> I'm trying to wrap my head around these changes a bit. It looks like in the main branch, @mtrmac has moved most of the changes to containers-lib with [containers/container-libs#765](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/765).
> 
> Is there a reason to do the changes here in Skopeo rather than backporting [containers/container-libs#765](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/765) into the container-libs podman-5.8 branch?

~All of the proxy _code_ was moved from Skopeo to container-libs since the last release. So, patching the proxy directly on the branch, and patching container-libs on main, should be quite similar changes, a “backport” if you will.

To use the unchanged code from `main`, we would need to backport the move of the code from Skopeo to container-libs (in both repos), and vendor an updated c/common into Skopeo.

I think changing the code directly is much easier, and a bit lower-risk.

My main concern is getting into some whacky upgrade issue between 1.22 and 1.23.  As long we cover that later, and everyone else is comfortable with this as is,
LGTM

The tests are still in heavy flake mode.  I have just restarted again.  @mtrmac, if you feel comfortable merging this with the tests as they are, please do.

Re: [those `Failed to start: INTERNAL: Compute engine internal error! null`][1], possibly that's:

> *[Instance failed to start!](https://cirrus-ci.org/faq/#instance-failed-to-start)*
> It means that Cirrus CI has made a successful API call to a [computing service](https://cirrus-ci.org/guide/supported-computing-services/) to allocate resources. But a requested resource wasn't created.
>
> If it happened for an OSS project, please contact [support](https://cirrus-ci.org/support/) immediately. Otherwise check your cloud console first and then contact [support](https://cirrus-ci.org/support/) if it's still not clear what happened.

Are we an OSS project that needs to open a support case?

[1]: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2847#issuecomment-4238800427

I'm not sure of our OSS determination at this point, but given the upcoming changes to cirrus, I don't think it matters at this point.  Just two tests remaining that are unhappy.  I'll see if I can get a hold of @mtrmac
For the record, the corresponding mainline change (with even better tests, thanks to @cgwalters ) is https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/766 .
